KIND REMINDER v2 — SYSTEM SPECIFICATION
Single-machine cron job orchestration engine. Go + SQLite (WAL). No external dependencies.
HTTP API on port 8080. Bearer token auth on all endpoints except GET /health.

CORE MODEL
  Provider  = delivery credentials (telegram bot_token / SMTP config / webhook base_url)
  Channel   = delivery target (chat_id / email to / webhook url) + optional provider_id
  Job       = cron schedule + ordered Steps[]
  Step      = atomic execution unit (shell | webhook | notification)
  Execution = one job run instance (status: running | success | failed)
  StepRun   = one step execution result within an Execution

EXECUTION SEMANTICS
  - Steps execute serially in order_index ASC order
  - Each step: up to step.retry attempts (default 1 = no retry)
  - Retry backoff: attempt 1→2s, 2→5s, 3→10s (fixed)
  - Step timeout enforced via context cancel (default 300s if not set)
  - StepResult.Status determines success/failure (never infer from Error string)
  - If step fails AND continue_on_error=false → execution marked failed, remaining steps skipped
  - If step fails AND continue_on_error=true → failure recorded, execution continues
  - Template render failure → step status=failed, error written to execution_step.error

CONCURRENCY
  - Same job: at most 1 running execution at any time (DB partial unique index enforces this)
  - INDEX: UNIQUE ON executions(job_id) WHERE status='running' AND job_id != '__adhoc__'
  - job_id='__adhoc__' (POST /send): multiple concurrent executions allowed
  - Jobs dispatch via in-memory worker pool (default 10 workers)
  - Scheduler tick: 30s, evaluates next_run_at <= current_minute (truncated to minute)
  - Missed runs: skipped and fast-forwarded to next future slot (no catch-up replay, max_lateness=1m default)

TEMPLATE SYSTEM
  Engine: Go text/template
  Context keys:
    .job   → *model.Job  {ID, CronExpr, Timezone, Title, Enabled, NextRunAt, LastRunAt, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt}
    .steps → map[string]StepResult  keyed by step_id
    .now   → time.Time
  StepResult fields:
    .Status   string  "success" | "failed"
    .ExitCode int     (shell only; 0 for other types)
    .Stdout   string  (truncated to 8192 bytes)
    .Stderr   string  (truncated to 8192 bytes)
    .Error    string  system-level error message
  Access pattern: (index .steps "step_id").Stdout
  Error handling: parse/execute error → step failed, error in execution_step.error, no panic

DATA TYPES

  Job {
    id         string    UUID
    cron       string    5-field cron expression
    timezone   string    IANA timezone (e.g. "Asia/Shanghai")
    title      string
    enabled    bool
    next_run_at time
    last_run_at time      (omitempty)
    created_at  time
    updated_at  time
    steps      []Step    (included in GET /jobs/:id and POST response; omitted in GET /jobs list)
  }

  Step {
    id               string  UUID (generated server-side)
    job_id           string  (omitempty in responses)
    step_id          string  business identifier, UNIQUE per job, used in template references
    order_index      int     execution order, UNIQUE per job
    type             string  "shell" | "webhook" | "notification"
    config           object  type-specific config (see STEP CONFIGS)
    timeout          int     seconds; 0 = use default 300s
    retry            int     max attempts; 0 or 1 = no retry
    continue_on_error bool   default false
  }

  Execution {
    id             string
    job_id         string     "__adhoc__" for POST /send executions
    scheduled_at   time|null  cron-calculated time; null for manual/adhoc triggers
    triggered_at   time       when execution actually started
    started_at     time       (omitempty)
    finished_at    time       (omitempty)
    status         string     "running" | "success" | "failed"
    trigger_type   string     "cron" | "manual" | "adhoc"
    triggered_by   string     (omitempty) "system" for cron, "api" for manual trigger
    error          string     (omitempty)
    steps          []ExecutionStep
  }

  ExecutionStep {
    id           string
    execution_id string  (omitempty)
    step_id      string
    type         string
    status       string  "success" | "failed"
    started_at   time    (omitempty)
    finished_at  time    (omitempty)
    exit_code    int     (omitempty)
    stdout       string  (omitempty, truncated 8KB)
    stderr       string  (omitempty, truncated 8KB)
    error        string  (omitempty)
  }

  Provider {
    id         string
    type       string  "telegram" | "email" | "webhook"
    config     object  type-specific (see PROVIDER CONFIGS)
    created_at time
  }

  Channel {
    id          string
    type        string  "telegram" | "email" | "webhook"
    provider_id string  (omitempty; if absent, uses global config.yaml fallback)
    config      object  type-specific (see CHANNEL CONFIGS)
    created_at  time
  }

STEP CONFIGS

  shell config:
    { "script": "/app/scripts/name.sh" }
    script must be absolute path; container must have file at that path
    exit_code != 0 → failed
    stdout/stderr captured and stored

  webhook config:
    {
      "method": "POST",              (default "POST")
      "url": "https://...",          (required; absolute URL)
      "headers": { "K": "V|template" },
      "body_template": "string|template"
    }
    headers values and body_template are rendered via text/template before sending
    HTTP status >= 400 → failed
    response body stored in stdout (truncated 8KB)
    client timeout: 10s (not overridden by step.timeout in current impl)

  notification config:
    {
      "channels": ["channel_id", ...],    (required; must be pre-created channels)
      "message_template": "string|template",  (required)
      "title_template": "string|template"     (optional; overrides Job.Title; empty = use Job.Title)
    }
    message_template and title_template rendered via text/template
    sends to all listed channels; last error wins if multiple fail

PROVIDER CONFIGS

  telegram: { "bot_token": "string" }
  email:    { "host": "string", "port": int, "user": "string", "pass": "string", "from": "string" }
  webhook:  { "base_url": "string", "timeout_seconds": int }

CHANNEL CONFIGS

  telegram: { "chat_id": "string" }   (string, not int, even for numeric IDs)
  email:    { "to": "string" }
  webhook:  { "url": "string" }       (absolute URL or path relative to provider base_url)

API ENDPOINTS

  GET  /health          → 200 "ok"  (no auth)
  GET  /stats           → Stats object:
                          {
                            "scheduler_running":   bool,    running state of the scheduler
                            "jobs_total":          int,     count of enabled jobs
                            "queue_size":          int,     current pending items in dispatch queue
                            "workers":             int,     number of worker goroutines
                            "queue_type":          string,  "memory" (only supported type)
                            "rate_limit_per_sec":  int,     0 = unlimited
                            "telegram_ok":         bool,    reachability check via getMe API
                            "smtp_ok":             bool,    TCP dial check to configured host:port
                            "webhook_ok":          bool,    HEAD/GET /health check to base_url
                            "last_execution_at":   time,    (omitempty) last finished execution time
                            "failures_last_hour":  int      count of failed executions in past 60 min
                          }
  
  POST   /providers     body: {id, type, config}              → 201 {id}
  GET    /providers     →  []Provider
  DELETE /providers/:id → 204

  POST   /channels      body: {id, type, provider_id?, config} → 201 {id}
  GET    /channels      → []Channel
  DELETE /channels/:id  → 204

  POST   /jobs          body: {cron, timezone, title, enabled, steps[]}  → 201 {id}
  GET    /jobs          → []Job  (steps field omitted)
  GET    /jobs/:id      → Job (with steps[])
  PATCH  /jobs/:id      body: {cron?, timezone?, title?, enabled?, steps?[]}  → 200 Job
                        steps field: if present → full replacement of all steps
                        steps field: if absent  → steps unchanged
  DELETE /jobs/:id      → 204  (cascades to job_steps)

  GET  /executions      params: limit(default 50, max 500), offset(default 0),
                                job_id(filter), include_adhoc(bool, default false)
                        sorted by triggered_at DESC
                        → []Execution (each includes steps[])

  GET  /executions/:id  → Execution (with steps[])
                        use after POST /jobs/:id/trigger to inspect stdout/stderr

  POST /jobs/:id/trigger  optional headers: Idempotency-Key
                          optional query: wait=true, timeout=N (seconds, default 30)
                          optional body (max 64KB): {override: {step_id: {config_key: val}}}
                          → 201 {execution_id, status: "running", trigger: "manual"}
                          → 200 {execution_id, status: "success"|"failed", trigger: "manual"} (wait=true, finished)
                          → 202 {execution_id, status: "running", trigger: "manual"} (wait=true, timed out)
                          → 409 {error: "job already running"} (concurrent run prevented)
                          → 200 {execution_id, ...} (Idempotency-Key reuse — returns existing execution)
                          Does NOT modify next_run_at; cron schedule unaffected.
                          override: merges into step config JSON only; blocked fields: type, order_index, step_id; null values ignored

  POST /send            body: {timezone?, steps: [{step_id, order_index, type, config, timeout?, retry?, continue_on_error?}]}
                        timezone: IANA string; affects .now and time rendering in all templates for this execution
                        → 200 {execution_id, status, steps: []ExecutionStep}
                        job_id internally set to "__adhoc__"
                        synchronous: waits for all steps to complete before responding

ERROR RESPONSES
  All errors: {"error": "message string"}
  400 Bad Request   — validation failure, unknown fields (DisallowUnknownFields)
  401 Unauthorized  — missing or wrong Bearer token
  404 Not Found     — resource does not exist
  500 Internal      — server/db error

SCHEDULER BEHAVIOR
  - tick interval: 30s
  - due condition: next_run_at <= floor(now, minute)
  - dispatch: INSERT execution(status=running, job_id, scheduled_at) — conflicts on unique constraints → skip
  - after dispatch: advance job next_run_at, last_run_at
  - NO-CATCHUP MODE (default): missed runs are never replayed
      lateness = now.In(jobTZ) - scheduledAt.In(jobTZ)
      if lateness > max_lateness (default 1m):
          FastForwardJobNextRun to cron.Next(now)  [guaranteed > now via loop]
          return without creating execution or running any step
  - Applies both on startup (stale next_run_at in DB) and during normal ticks
  - Lateness computed in job's own timezone (DST-safe)
  - next_run_at fast-forward always uses cron.Next(now), never cron.Next(old_scheduled_at)
  - job with no steps: execution completes immediately as success

DATABASE TABLES
  jobs              (id, cron_expr, timezone, title, enabled, next_run_at, last_run_at, created_at, updated_at)
  job_steps         (id, job_id FK, step_id, order_index, type, config_json, timeout, retry, continue_on_error)
                    UNIQUE(job_id, step_id), UNIQUE(job_id, order_index)
  executions        (id, job_id, scheduled_at, started_at, finished_at, status, error,
                     trigger_type, triggered_at, triggered_by, idempotency_key)
                    scheduled_at nullable (NULL for manual/adhoc)
                    UNIQUE(job_id, idempotency_key) WHERE idempotency_key IS NOT NULL
  execution_steps   (id, execution_id FK, step_id, type, status, started_at, finished_at, exit_code, stdout, stderr, error)
  channels          (id, type, provider_id, config, created_at)
  providers         (id, type, config, created_at)
  schema_migrations (version, applied_at)
  INDEX: uniq_running_job ON executions(job_id) WHERE status='running' AND job_id != '__adhoc__'

TYPICAL USAGE PATTERNS

  Pattern 1: Shell script + notification on result
    Step 1 (shell):        run /app/scripts/backup.sh
    Step 2 (notification): message_template references (index .steps "backup").Stdout

  Pattern 2: Webhook with dynamic body from previous step
    Step 1 (shell):   run script, capture output
    Step 2 (webhook): body_template uses (index .steps "step1").ExitCode

  Pattern 3: POST /send from CI pipeline
    POST /send with notification step → synchronous response with result

  Pattern 4: continue_on_error for cleanup steps
    Step 1: main task (continue_on_error=false)
    Step 2: cleanup/notify (continue_on_error=true, always runs even if step 1 fails)

CONFIG FILE (config.yaml)
  server_port:  string  (default "8080")
  db_path:      string  (default "./reminder.db")
  api_token:    string  REQUIRED
  telegram.bot_token: string   (global fallback)
  smtp.host/port/user/pass/from: string/int/string/string/string
  scheduler.workers:   int
  scheduler.queue_size: int
  scheduler.max_lateness: duration  (default "1m"; missed runs older than this are skipped, never replayed)
  queue.type:          "memory"
  queue.workers:       int
  queue.size:          int
  queue.rate_limit_per_sec: int
  webhook.enabled:     bool
  webhook.base_url:    string
  webhook.timeout_seconds: int

ENV OVERRIDES (higher priority than config.yaml)
  API_TOKEN, DB_PATH, SERVER_PORT, CONFIG_FILE,
  TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS, SMTP_FROM,
  QUEUE_TYPE, QUEUE_WORKERS, QUEUE_SIZE, RATE_LIMIT_PER_SEC,
  WEBHOOK_ENABLED, WEBHOOK_BASE_URL, WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS

KNOWN LIMITATIONS (current impl)
  - webhook: client timeout hardcoded to 10s; step.timeout 对 webhook 无效
      (step.timeout still cancels the context, but http.Client.Timeout=10s fires first)
  - notification: 多 channel 失败时 last error wins，无 per-channel 结果
  - shell: 仅支持 absolute path，不支持 inline script
  - stdout/stderr: 硬截断 8192 bytes，不可配置
  - webhook body_template: 纯字符串拼接，JSON 需手动转义
  - GET /executions: offset-based pagination only; no cursor; large offsets are slow on big datasets
  - no distributed scheduling; single-node only

PLANNED / NOT YET IMPLEMENTED
  - webhook body_template: JSON object mode to avoid manual escape of inner quotes
  - per-step retry policy with configurable backoff intervals
  - GET /executions cursor-based pagination
  - per-channel result in notification step (currently last-error-wins)

TRIGGER TYPES (v3)
  cron    scheduled via cron expression, triggered by scheduler
  manual  triggered via POST /jobs/:id/trigger; scheduled_at=null; does not touch next_run_at
  adhoc   triggered via POST /send; not tied to a job; job_id="__adhoc__"

  Execution audit fields:
    trigger_type  cron | manual | adhoc
    triggered_at  when execution actually started (scheduling latency = triggered_at - scheduled_at for cron)
    triggered_by  "system" for cron; "api" for manual trigger

  Idempotency:
    Header: Idempotency-Key
    Scoped: UNIQUE(job_id, idempotency_key) — same key across different jobs does not conflict
    Behavior: duplicate key returns existing execution (idempotent)
